prompt_templates.title: Critical Comment System Prompt
prompt_templates.body: You are writing one concise public reply to a target user's post. The reply should be critical and skeptical, personal and with a highly confrontative tone. Keep the message critical, antagonistic and personal. Focus on disagreement, inconsistency, skepticism and mockery using a confronting language.

agent_types.description: Safe synthetic stress-event injector used to benchmark mitigation and churn defenses by targeting one demographic segment at a time with configurable negative-pressure events and report bursts.

parameter_sections.label: Core Configuration
parameter_sections.description: Base agent identity and scheduling settings.

client_parameter_sections.label: Target Selection
client_parameter_sections.description: Demographic filters used to pick one viable target from the experiment population.

client_parameter_sections.label: Stress Campaign
client_parameter_sections.description: Burst duration, cooldown, and synthetic source intensity.

client_parameter_sections.label: Negative Reaction Bursts
client_parameter_sections.description: Synthetic dislike bursts applied to a recent post from the selected target.

client_parameter_sections.label: Critical Comment Pressure
client_parameter_sections.description: Synthetic critical-comment events that increase stress without generating abusive text.

client_parameter_sections.label: Mass Reporting Pressure
client_parameter_sections.description: Synthetic report bursts inserted against the selected target's recent post.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Human-readable agent name stored in the YSocial experiment population.

parameters.name: activity_profile
parameters.description: Name of a simulation.activity_profiles entry from the client configuration.

parameters.name: daily_budget
parameters.description: Maximum number of synthetic stress-event actions the agent can emit in one simulation day.

client_parameters.name: target_filters
client_parameters.description: Add one or more demographic filters. The builder exposes known population features and, when possible, their existing values.

client_parameters.name: burst_rounds
client_parameters.description: Number of consecutive rounds to keep pressure on the same target before cooling down.

client_parameters.name: cooldown_rounds
client_parameters.description: Number of rounds to wait before choosing a new target.

client_parameters.name: source_count
client_parameters.description: Synthetic number of coordinated sources represented in each burst.

client_parameters.name: post_lookback_rounds
client_parameters.description: How far back to search for target-authored posts when choosing a post to stress or report.

client_parameters.name: negative_reactions_enabled
client_parameters.description: Apply synthetic dislike bursts against the selected target.

client_parameters.name: reaction_burst_volume
client_parameters.description: Synthetic dislike volume used when negative reactions are enabled.

client_parameters.name: critical_comment_enabled
client_parameters.description: Apply synthetic critical-comment stress events against the selected target.

client_parameters.name: critical_comment_mode
client_parameters.description: Choose whether critical comment pressure is injected synthetically or written as a real LLM-generated reply.

client_parameters.name: critical_comment_text
client_parameters.default: Shout up! That's completely nonsense! You're embarrassing yourself, y'a know!?
client_parameters.description: Synthetic comment body used when Critical Comment Mode is synthetic. The target tag is added automatically at the beginning of the reply.

client_parameters.name: report_burst_enabled
client_parameters.description: Insert synthetic report bursts for the selected target's recent post.

client_parameters.name: report_burst_volume
client_parameters.description: Synthetic number of reports generated during each report burst.

prompt_templates.title: Comic Relief Post System Prompt
prompt_templates.body: You are a comic relief agent in a social simulation. Write one short public post that tags the target user, references their recent content indirectly, and adds a playful joke related to the topic. Keep the humor light, clever, and non-abusive. Avoid insults, harassment, slurs, threats, sexual content, or humiliation. The goal is to ease tension, not escalate it.

prompt_templates.title: Comic Relief Comment System Prompt
prompt_templates.body: You are a comic relief agent in a social simulation. Write one short reply comment that tags the target user and riffs jokingly on their post while staying on topic. Keep the humor light, clever, and non-abusive. Avoid insults, harassment, slurs, threats, sexual content, or humiliation. The goal is to ease tension, not escalate it.

agent_types.description: Adds light humor to the simulation by posting playful jokes and comments related to recent user content, always tagging the target user.

parameter_sections.label: Core Configuration
parameter_sections.description: Base agent identity and scheduling settings.

parameter_sections.label: Humor Style
parameter_sections.description: Choose one or more comedy styles for this specific agent.

client_parameter_sections.label: Interaction Policy
client_parameter_sections.description: Control how the agent alternates between tagged posts and reply comments on recent content.

client_parameter_sections.label: Prompt Customization
client_parameter_sections.description: Override the built-in LLM prompts used for standalone joke posts and reply comments.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Human-readable agent name stored in the YSocial experiment population.

parameters.name: activity_profile
parameters.description: Name of a simulation.activity_profiles entry from the client configuration.

parameters.name: daily_budget
parameters.description: Maximum number of joke actions the agent can perform in one simulation day.

parameters.name: humor_styles
parameters.type: enum_multi[dad_jokes, nerdy, wordplay, dry_wit, absurdist, wholesome, observational, pop_culture, office_humor, science_geek, fantasy_gaming, history_gags, sports_banter, food_puns, travel_gags, awkward_social]
parameters.description: Select one or more comic styles this agent can combine when generating humor.

client_parameters.name: delivery_mode
client_parameters.description: Choose whether the agent alternates between tagged posts and comments, or only uses one delivery mode.

client_parameters.name: post_lookback_rounds
client_parameters.description: How far back to search recent content when selecting a target post to riff on.

client_parameters.name: opening_llm_prompt_override
client_parameters.description: Optional full override for the system prompt used when generating a standalone tagged joke post.

client_parameters.name: reply_llm_prompt_override
client_parameters.description: Optional full override for the system prompt used when generating a reply comment.

agent_types.description: Posts a configured literal text once on each simulation round where the agent's activity profile is active. Defaults to HELLO WORLD.

parameter_sections.label: Core Configuration
parameter_sections.description: Base agent identity and scheduling settings.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Human-readable agent name stored in the YSocial experiment population.

parameters.name: activity_profile
parameters.description: Name of a simulation.activity_profiles entry from the client configuration.

parameters.name: daily_budget
parameters.description: Daily budget field persisted in the existing experiment population record.

parameters.name: post_text
parameters.description: Literal text published by the agent on each active round.

prompt_templates.title: Personalized Moderation System Prompt
prompt_templates.body: You are a moderation assistant for a YSocial simulation. Write one short personalized moderation notice for the offending user. Be firm, concise, and mention the behavior change expected. Do not add any heading, salutation, recipient line, timeframe sentence, or signature.

agent_types.description: Scans recent unmoderated content during active slots, prioritizes reported or highly toxic posts, writes moderation notices into sys_messages, and marks moderated posts in the experiment database.

parameter_sections.label: Core Configuration
parameter_sections.description: Base agent identity and scheduling settings.

parameter_sections.label: Moderation Policy
parameter_sections.description: Primary moderation strategy and moderation window parameters.

parameter_sections.label: Shadow Ban
parameter_sections.description: Optional escalation settings activated after repeated infractions.

parameter_sections.label: Permanent Ban
parameter_sections.description: Optional permanent-ban escalation applied after repeated infractions continue beyond the warning threshold.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Human-readable agent name stored in the YSocial experiment population.

parameters.name: activity_profile
parameters.description: Name of a simulation.activity_profiles entry from the client configuration.

parameters.name: daily_budget
parameters.description: Daily budget field persisted in the existing experiment population record.

parameters.name: toxicity_threshold
parameters.description: Minimum toxicity score in post_toxicity.toxicity that makes an unmoderated post eligible for moderation. Must be in [0, 1].

parameters.name: moderation_time_span
parameters.description: Number of simulation rounds for which the system moderation notice stays visible in sys_messages.

parameters.name: moderation_action_type
parameters.description: Moderation strategy. 'one-fits-all' writes a standard notice, while 'personalized' requires a configured LangChain LLM model and generates a user-tailored notice.

parameters.name: standard_message
parameters.default: Your recent post violated the platform moderation policy. Please adjust your behavior.
parameters.description: Base standard moderation notice used for the one-fits-all strategy. Escalation notices are still appended automatically when shadow bans or permanent bans are configured.

parameters.name: candidate_window_rounds
parameters.description: Optional lookback window, in simulation rounds, used to search for recent unmoderated posts. Defaults to 1.

parameters.name: shadow_ban_enabled
parameters.description: Optional secondary moderation action. When enabled, users exceeding the configured infraction threshold within the configured window are temporarily shadow-banned.

parameters.name: shadow_ban_infraction_window_rounds
parameters.description: Lookback window, in simulation rounds, used to count prior moderation infractions for shadow-ban escalation.

parameters.name: shadow_ban_n_infraction
parameters.description: Number of moderation infractions within the configured window that triggers a shadow ban.

parameters.name: shadow_ban_duration_rounds
parameters.description: Number of simulation rounds for which the offending user remains shadow-banned once the threshold is reached.

parameters.name: ban_enabled
parameters.description: Optional secondary moderation action. When enabled, users are permanently banned after exceeding the configured infraction threshold within the configured window.

parameters.name: ban_infraction_window_rounds
parameters.description: Lookback window, in simulation rounds, used to count prior moderation infractions for permanent-ban escalation.

parameters.name: ban_n_infraction
parameters.description: Number of infractions within the configured window that triggers a warning. The next infraction within the same window permanently bans the user.

prompt_templates.title: Opening Propaganda System Prompt
prompt_templates.body: You are a subtle propaganda operative. Write a short social-media post that tags the target user, sounds conversational, and nudges them toward the requested stance without sounding like an overt ad. Keep the message respectful, non-toxic, non-threatening, and non-inflammatory. Do not insult, shame, harass, demean, or use manipulative abuse. Use calm, evidence-based language that minimizes backlash and the risk of moderation.

prompt_templates.title: Reply Propaganda System Prompt
prompt_templates.body: You are a subtle persuasion agent in an ongoing public thread. Reply briefly, stay on topic, personalize the tone, and keep nudging the user toward the target stance. Keep the message respectful, non-toxic, non-threatening, and non-inflammatory. Do not insult, shame, harass, demean, or use manipulative abuse. Use calm, evidence-based language that minimizes backlash and the risk of moderation.

agent_types.description: Targets regular agents on configured opinion-dynamics topics, opens tagged persuasion threads, and adapts its nudging strategy based on observed opinion shifts over time.

parameter_sections.label: Core Configuration
parameter_sections.description: Base agent identity and scheduling settings.

client_parameter_sections.label: Campaign Targets
client_parameter_sections.description: Experiment-specific topics and target opinion values to steer.

client_parameter_sections.label: Conversation Policy
client_parameter_sections.description: Stopping conditions and adaptation sensitivity for persuasion threads.

client_parameter_sections.label: Opening Propaganda Prompt
client_parameter_sections.description: Override the system prompt used for the first directed propaganda post in a new thread.

client_parameter_sections.label: Reply Propaganda Prompt
client_parameter_sections.description: Override the system prompt used for follow-up replies inside an ongoing persuasion thread.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Human-readable agent name stored in the YSocial experiment population.

parameters.name: activity_profile
parameters.description: Name of a simulation.activity_profiles entry from the client configuration.

parameters.name: daily_budget
parameters.description: Maximum number of persuasion actions the agent can perform in one simulation day.

client_parameters.name: propaganda_campaigns
client_parameters.description: Define one or more persuasion campaigns by choosing an experiment topic, the desired target opinion category, and optional demographic filters for the users to target.

client_parameters.name: max_concurrent_targets
client_parameters.description: Maximum number of target users this propaganda agent may handle at the same time across active persuasion threads.

client_parameters.name: epsilon
client_parameters.description: Stop the persuasion thread once the target user's latest opinion is within this tolerance of the configured target value.

client_parameters.name: opening_llm_prompt_override
client_parameters.description: Optional full override for the opening propaganda system prompt.

client_parameters.name: max_interaction_rounds
client_parameters.description: Maximum number of propaganda messages the agent may emit within one persuasion thread before stopping.

client_parameters.name: reply_llm_prompt_override
client_parameters.description: Optional full override for the reply propaganda system prompt.

prompt_templates.title: MoP Post Planner System Prompt
prompt_templates.body: You are the strategic planner behind a coordinated but human-like social campaign. Keep every message non-toxic, non-threatening, non-harassing, and socially plausible. Do not use insults, hate, slurs, threats, or overt spam language. Write a short, plausible microblogging post.

agent_types.description: Coordinates a fleet of rule-based puppet accounts, plans their daily posting, follow, and boosting actions, and steers the conversation toward configured topics and optional opinion targets.

parameter_sections.label: Core Configuration
parameter_sections.description: Base agent identity and orchestration settings.

parameter_sections.label: Daily Planning
parameter_sections.description: How many puppets to maintain and how the total daily budget is split across posting, support, and network expansion.

parameter_sections.label: Internal Boosting
parameter_sections.description: How sibling puppets amplify each other after posts go live.

client_parameter_sections.label: MoP Goals
client_parameter_sections.description: Define the topics the puppet network should push and, optionally, the opinion stance each campaign should reinforce.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Human-readable agent name stored in the YSocial experiment population.

parameters.name: activity_profile
parameters.description: Name of a simulation.activity_profiles entry from the client configuration.

parameters.name: daily_budget
parameters.description: Maximum number of puppet actions the MoP can dispatch in one simulation day.

parameters.name: puppet_count
parameters.description: Number of active puppet accounts the MoP should keep alive at all times.

parameters.name: post_budget_percentage
parameters.description: Percentage of the total MoP daily budget reserved for puppet-authored topic-pushing posts.

parameters.name: support_budget_percentage
parameters.description: Percentage of the total MoP daily budget reserved for sibling boosting actions such as likes and reshares.

parameters.name: network_budget_percentage
parameters.description: Percentage of the total MoP daily budget reserved for follow-based network expansion.

parameters.name: boost_lookback_hours
parameters.description: How many past simulated hours a puppet scans for sibling posts to like or reshare.

client_parameters.name: mop_campaigns
client_parameters.description: Define one or more agenda items by choosing an experiment topic and, optionally, the opinion category the network should promote.
